overview: This U/B/R Vintage deck leverages cheap card advantage spells to rapidly assemble threats and control the board. By cycling through the library with Paradoxical Outcome and Stock Up, it maintains relentless pressure while disrupting opponent plans. Thundertrap Trainer provides a resilient body that fuels further card draw, creating a snowball effect that overwhelms opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Overwhelming board presence with Thundertrap Trainer and recurring threats
Card advantage exhaustion via continuous draw spells
Direct damage or value grinding in the late game

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two cheap draw spells
Hands containing Thundertrap Trainer and a one-drop
Hands with early disruption or land drops
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Paradoxical Outcome, and early interaction (Thoughtseize, Duress, Lightning Axe, or Izzet Charm).
Keep hands with Thundertrap Trainer if supported by a land and a cantrip/answer.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking mana acceleration or draw
Hands without a clear path to turn one or two
Mulligan pure land draws, hands with only Paradoxical Outcome and no lands, or hands with no early interaction and no threats.

early_game: Play lands and cast cheap draw spells to establish card advantage
Deploy Thundertrap Trainer early to generate value and board presence
Use Paradoxical Outcome to filter for key pieces or disrupt opponent's opening
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize/Duress if possible, or hold Paradoxical Outcome for later.
Turn 2: Play a shockland, cast Lightning Axe or Izzet Charm for tempo, or cast Thundertrap Trainer if ahead.
Sequence lands to ensure blue/red/black access for Paradoxical Outcome and interaction.
mid_game: Maintain board control while cycling through the deck for answers
Leverage Stock Up to refill the hand and apply continuous pressure
Trade efficiently to preserve resources for the late game
use it to find answers when low on resources, not just to cycle.
Stock Up is for late-game card advantage when you have a board or need to stabilize.
Manage mana to keep open for interaction and Paradoxical Outcome's cycling.
Commit Thundertrap Trainer when you can protect it or when you have enough interaction to survive the turn.
Hold back if you're low on answers or facing a heavy removal suite.
late_game: Use accumulated card advantage to outvalue opponents
Recycle threats from the graveyard using Paradoxical Outcome
Close out the game with relentless draw and board presence
Thundertrap Trainer in combat, often supported by Izzet Charm/Lightning Axe to clear blockers or protect.
Paradoxical Outcome to cycle into a win condition or extra turns.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to combat, enough interaction to survive, or when the opponent is out of answers.
Commit when you can safely attack with Thundertrap Trainer and have a backup plan.

key_cards.name: Paradoxical Outcome
key_cards.notes: Cast early to dig for combo pieces or answers; use the graveyard exile clause to fuel recursion strategies

key_cards.name: Stock Up
key_cards.notes: Play to refill the hand after committing resources; pairs well with mana rocks to enable turn two plays

key_cards.name: Thundertrap Trainer
key_cards.notes: Deploy as a board presence that synergizes with draw spells; use its abilities to maintain pressure while cycling the deck

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting draw spells before committing to threats to ensure hand size
Use Paradoxical Outcome to exile opponent's key cards if possible
Save Stock Up for turns when you need to refill after a board wipe or combat
Cycle lands and cheap spells to find win conditions faster
Paradoxical Outcome can be used to cycle into itself for extra draws.
Stock Up is a late-game win condition in control mirrors.
Watch for graveyard hate against Dredge.
Thundertrap Trainer benefits from combat tricks and protection.

matchups.advice: Focus on drawing past removal and applying early pressure with Thundertrap Trainer
Disrupt their casting sequence with early draw and graveyard interaction
Use draw spells to find removal and stabilize with resilient threats

Use Paradoxical Outcome to cycle into cheaper threats or answers. Use Lightning Axe/Izzet Charm for instant-speed interaction. Don't overcommit to expensive spells; use cantrips and cheap interaction.
Use discard to hit key pieces, Lightning Axe to remove ramp targets, and Paradoxical Outcome to find answers. Stabilize quickly with interaction and threats.
Win the discard war. Use Paradoxical Outcome to find answers and threats. Track graveyard and hand size. Win by out-resourcing and better sequencing.

common_threats: Watch for Lurrus of the Dream-Den triggers, Jewel Shops mana rocks, Dredge engines, Oath targets, Initiative triggers.
Track graveyard hate availability, countermagic, and removal suites.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Paradoxical Outcome, Stock Up, Thundertrap Trainer
